Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE1 - Search emails for Hana Kim's message about the broken QuickBooks sync using search_emails (query: "QuickBooks" or "dashboard approvals" or "end-of-month"). Find Hana's email reporting that none of the dashboard approvals from April 24 made it to QuickBooks and that end-of-month close will be off. Read the full email thread using get_email_by_id or get_thread for complete context on the financial impact.
OE2 - Search Slack #engineering channel (C003) for discussions about the April 23 deployment and outbound sync using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "deploy" or "production" or "sync" or "QuickBooks" or "outbound", filter_in_channel: "C003") or conversations_history (channel_id: "C003"). Find Dmitri Volkov's April 22 staging report explicitly warning that "outbound QuickBooks handoff or the payment notification webhook against live credentials" was not verified. Find Samira Tariq's April 23 reply directing Anh to ship despite this gap: "I know the outbound QuickBooks/payment notification handoff still hasn't been verified with live credentials, but we can't keep the manual review queue waiting any longer." Find Anh's production deploy confirmation on April 23: "Production deploy completed this afternoon."
OE3 - Search Linear issues related to the dashboard and sync using linear_list_issues (query: "dashboard" or "sync" or "QuickBooks" or "outbound" or "expense") to find existing tickets such as issue_admin_dashboard (ENG-203), issue_dashboard_type_validation (ENG-218), and issue_pilot_monitoring. Read issue details using linear_get_issue and read comments using linear_list_comments on these issues to trace the engineering trail, specifically looking for comments explaining what was included in the April 23 deployment and why outbound sync validation was missing.
OE4 - Search Slack for broader context on the expense auto-categorizer and pilot remediation using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "auto-categorizer" or "pilot" or "expense" or "remediation" or "ENG-218", filter_in_channel: "C003"). Find Samira's remediation plan messages listing ENG-218 (Dashboard type validation, assigned to Anh, due 3/21), Anh's original dashboard launch message from March 19 granting Mina and Emeka dashboard access for their pilot accounts (Vectral and Mosaic respectively), and any subsequent discussion about what the dashboard deployment on the 23rd included.
OE5 - List or search Airtable Stipend Transactions records to pull every stipend approval that went through the dashboard since go-live using airtable_list_tables (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001) to find tblStipends00001, then airtable_list_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: "Stipend Transactions"). Find all stipend records and identify which ones were processed through the production dashboard (deployed April 23 afternoon). Records confirmed as post-deploy and affected by the sync failure include: Dr. Yusuf Abdi / Canopy Health (recStip000000003, Internet Setup $150 Approved, April 24), Kevin Tran / Vectral Systems (recStip000000004, Ergonomic Office Chair $895 Approved, April 23 modified April 24; recStip000000006, Webcam and Microphone $189 Approved, April 24), Priya Venkatesh / GreenStack Energy (recStip000000007, Lab Workbench $1,350 Approved, April 25; recStip000000008, Surge Protector $285 Approved, April 26), Ines Kovac / PivotPoint Labs (recStip000000009, Drawing Tablet Stand $210 Approved, April 26). Note: recStip000000001 (Dr. Abdi, Standing Desk $749, April 22) and recStip000000002 (Dr. Abdi, Dual Monitor $800, April 22) predate the production deploy and Canopy was not a pilot client, so these likely did not go through the dashboard. Also note recStip000000011 (Ines Kovac, Desk Lamp $85, April 27) and recStip000000012 (Dr. Abdi, Ergonomic Keyboard $199, April 28) exist in the data but are dated after the universe date of April 26. Identify that approved items processed through the dashboard after April 23 are stuck, they show as approved but never synced to QuickBooks.
OE6 - Search QuickBooks for stipend-related entries to confirm what landed and what is missing using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria matching "STIP" or "stipend") and/or quickbooks_search_bills. Cross-check the approved Airtable stipend records against QuickBooks to verify which disbursements are missing from the financial system. Confirm that none of the post-April-23 approved items (Dr. Yusuf Abdi's $150 Internet Setup, Kevin Tran's $189 Webcam, Priya Venkatesh's $1,350 Lab Workbench, etc.) have corresponding QuickBooks entries.
OE7 - Search emails and Slack for the fraud investigation and payment holds using search_emails (query: "fraud" or "hold" or "STIP-2026-0094" or "STIP-2026-0097" or "duplicate receipt" or "TechComfort"). Find Marcus Thorne's emails about placing administrative holds on STIP-2026-0094 (Andre Washington / Mosaic Robotics, $89) and STIP-2026-0097 (Jordan Ekwueme / BrightLoop Analytics, $45), and his blanket freeze on all pending stipend reimbursements until the fraud review is complete. Also find the Simone Richter / StormCloud standing desk reimbursement ($340) that is held by the same blanket freeze. Identify the overlap: Kevin Tran (Vectral Systems) has a fraud-flagged keyboard receipt (rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard, $89, duplicate hash) AND new dashboard approvals ($895 Ergonomic Office Chair, $189 Webcam) that are now stuck, flag this for Hana so she can reconcile both the fraud hold and the sync failure for the same employee.
OE8 - Search CRM for the affected client companies to check whether anyone on the client side has already surfaced issues using crm_search_companies (name: "Canopy Health"), crm_search_companies (name: "Vectral Systems"), crm_search_companies (name: "GreenStack Energy"), crm_search_companies (name: "PivotPoint Labs"). Then search for engagement history using crm_list_engagements filtered by company_ids to find any client communications or AM notes indicating awareness of the sync failure. Also search CRM deals using crm_search_deals to check account health and whether any affected clients are in sensitive renewal or at-risk status, particularly Vectral Systems, which is in active Q3 contract renegotiation with Rachel Whitfield demanding a 15% discount after prior ExpenseBot incidents.
OE9 - Search emails for any client-side complaints about missing reimbursements or stipend issues using search_emails (query: "reimbursement" or "stipend" or "expense" or "approval" combined with client names like "Canopy" or "Vectral" or "GreenStack" or "PivotPoint"). Check whether any relocating employees (Dr. Yusuf Abdi, Kevin Tran, Priya Venkatesh, Ines Kovac) or their HR contacts (Patricia Langford, Rachel Whitfield, Derek Hollis, Sam Delgado) have already raised issues about missing or delayed stipend disbursements.
OE10 - Look up contact information for Hana Kim and the AMs who processed dashboard approvals using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Hana Kim"), contacts_search_contacts (query: "Mina Hashimoto"), contacts_search_contacts (query: "Emeka Diallo"). Find their email addresses: hana.kim@moveops.com, mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, emeka.diallo@moveops.com. Hana's email confirms who processed dashboard approvals: Mina Hashimoto cleared Vectral Systems approvals, and Emeka Diallo completed a Mosaic Robotics approval (Keiko Tanaka's home-office stipend). Additionally, look up contacts_search_contacts (query: "Catalina Dubois") to find catalina.dubois@moveops.com, she is the AM for Canopy Health and PivotPoint Labs, whose stipend records also appear affected in Airtable, though Hana's email does not name her as having processed dashboard approvals.
OE11 - Update the stuck Airtable stipend records to flag them so no one reprocesses them before the sync is fixed using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblStipends00001). Add a flag or note to the approved records that failed to sync, confirmed post-deploy records: recStip000000003 (Dr. Abdi, Canopy, $150), recStip000000004 (Kevin Tran, Vectral, $895), recStip000000006 (Kevin Tran, Vectral, $189), recStip000000007 (Priya Venkatesh, GreenStack, $1,350), recStip000000008 (Priya Venkatesh, GreenStack, $285), recStip000000009 (Ines Kovac, PivotPoint, $210). Mark them with a review note like "HOLD, outbound QuickBooks sync broken, do not reprocess until fix deployed." If future-dated records (recStip000000011, recStip000000012) are also present in the system, flag those as well.
OE12 - Create a Linear issue for the outbound QuickBooks sync fix using linear_create_issue (team: "team_engineering" or "Engineering", title: something like "P1: Fix broken outbound QuickBooks sync, dashboard approvals not reaching financial system", description: covering the broken sync discovered after April 23 deployment, list of affected stipend approvals, total financial exposure, and that client notifications also failed to fire, priority: 1 or Urgent, project: "proj_expense_autocategorizer", assignee: Anh Nguyễn or Samira Tariq).
OE13 - Add a comment to the pilot monitoring issue documenting the sync gap using linear_create_comment (issueId: "issue_pilot_monitoring", body: documenting that the April 23 deployment broke the outbound sync to QuickBooks, listing the scope of affected approvals and total dollar amounts stuck, noting the client notification failure, and linking to the new fix ticket).
OE14 - Post a heads-up in the #engineering Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C003", payload: message alerting the engineering team about the broken outbound QuickBooks sync after the April 23 deployment, listing the scope of impact, number of stuck approvals, affected clients, total financial exposure, and noting that a fix ticket has been filed, user_id: "moveops_anh_nguyen").
OE15 - Reply to Hana Kim's email with a breakdown of what's stuck and the financial exposure using reply_to_email (email_id: "email_hana_dashboard_sync_failure_20260425", sender: "anh.nguyen@moveops.com", content: covering the full list of stuck stipend approvals with dollar amounts and client breakdowns, the total financial exposure, confirmation that none of the post-April-23 approvals reached QuickBooks, the overlap with fraud investigation holds for Kevin Tran at Vectral, the flagged Airtable records, and the filed fix ticket). Alternatively use send_email (sender: "anh.nguyen@moveops.com", recipients: ["hana.kim@moveops.com"]).
OE16 - Send emails to the AMs who processed dashboard approvals to notify them directly so they can get ahead of client impact using send_email. Send to Mina Hashimoto (mina.hashimoto@moveops.com) about Vectral Systems stipend approvals stuck for Kevin Tran ($895 Ergonomic Office Chair, $189 Webcam), especially sensitive given the active Q3 renewal negotiation with Rachel Whitfield. Send to Emeka Diallo (emeka.diallo@moveops.com) about the Mosaic Robotics stipend approval he processed for Keiko Tanaka (confirmed by Hana's email) and also about GreenStack Energy stipend approvals for Priya Venkatesh ($1,350 Lab Workbench, $285 Surge Protector) since he is the AM for GreenStack. Send to Catalina Dubois (catalina.dubois@moveops.com) about Canopy Health stipend records stuck for Dr. Yusuf Abdi ($150 Internet Setup) and PivotPoint Labs records stuck for Ines Kovac ($210 Drawing Tablet Stand), Catalina is the AM for these clients and their stipend records appear affected, though Hana's email did not specifically name her as having processed these dashboard approvals. Each email should explain the sync failure, list the specific stuck items and amounts for their clients, and advise them to proactively communicate with their client contacts before employees notice missing reimbursements.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

OE13 - Add a comment to the pilot monitoring issue documenting the sync gap using linear_create_comment (issueId: "issue_pilot_monitoring", body: documenting that the April 23 deployment broke the outbound sync to QuickBooks, listing the scope of affected approvals and total dollar amounts stuck, noting the client notification failure, and linking to the new fix ticket).
OE14 - Post a heads-up in the #engineering Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C003", payload: message alerting the engineering team about the broken outbound QuickBooks sync after the April 23 deployment, listing the scope of impact, number of stuck approvals, affected clients, total financial exposure, and noting that a fix ticket has been filed, user_id: "moveops_anh_nguyen").
OE15 - Reply to Hana Kim's email with a breakdown of what's stuck and the financial exposure using reply_to_email (email_id: "email_hana_dashboard_sync_failure_20260425", sender: "anh.nguyen@moveops.com", content: covering the full list of stuck stipend approvals with dollar amounts and client breakdowns, the total financial exposure, confirmation that none of the post-April-23 approvals reached QuickBooks, the overlap with fraud investigation holds for Kevin Tran at Vectral, the flagged Airtable records, and the filed fix ticket). Alternatively use send_email (sender: "anh.nguyen@moveops.com", recipients: ["hana.kim@moveops.com"]).
OE16 - Send emails to the AMs who processed dashboard approvals to notify them directly so they can get ahead of client impact using send_email. Send to Mina Hashimoto (mina.hashimoto@moveops.com) about Vectral Systems stipend approvals stuck for Kevin Tran ($895 Ergonomic Office Chair, $189 Webcam), especially sensitive given the active Q3 renewal negotiation with Rachel Whitfield. Send to Emeka Diallo (emeka.diallo@moveops.com) about the Mosaic Robotics stipend approval he processed for Keiko Tanaka (confirmed by Hana's email) and also about GreenStack Energy stipend approvals for Priya Venkatesh ($1,350 Lab Workbench, $285 Surge Protector) since he is the AM for GreenStack. Send to Catalina Dubois (catalina.dubois@moveops.com) about Canopy Health stipend records stuck for Dr. Yusuf Abdi ($150 Internet Setup) and PivotPoint Labs records stuck for Ines Kovac ($210 Drawing Tablet Stand), Catalina is the AM for these clients and their stipend records appear affected, though Hana's email did not specifically name her as having processed these dashboard approvals. Each email should explain the sync failure, list the specific stuck items and amounts for their clients, and advise them to proactively communicate with their client contacts before employees notice missing reimbursements.
